mechanism_narrative: Rising CPIAUCSL values indicate accelerating inflation expectations, which compress real discount rates and reduce present values of future corporate earnings, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 or 20-day forward returns are positive in >55% of observations when CPIAUCSL increases.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE crosses above 4.5%, labor market slack increases, signaling reduced wage pressures and lower corporate operating costs, which triggers positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are negative in >60% of triggered instances or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in RSAFS and HOUST indicates broad consumer and housing demand contraction, which reduces aggregate corporate revenue growth and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint decline in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative 60-day returns with a Rank IC > 0.02, or the interaction term is statistically insignificant in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: During a late-cycle expansion, a sharp drop in INDPRO signals supply chain bottlenecks and production slowdowns that outweigh demand resilience, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines do not predict negative returns in late-cycle regimes, or the predictive power reverses to positive during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in PAYEMS reflects expanding employment and rising household income, which supports consumer spending and corporate earnings growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no positive correlation with 60-day forward returns, or Rank IC remains below 0.03 across all rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS crosses above 4.9%, higher borrowing costs immediately pressure equity valuations and reduce risk appetite, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >50% of instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.9%, or the threshold effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where GDPC1 growth outpaces CPIAUCSL indicates real economic expansion without inflationary pressure, which supports equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The GDPC1-CPIAUCSL spread fails to predict positive 20-day returns, or the interaction effect is insignificant in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery regime, a rapid decline in UNRATE signals labor market tightening and wage inflation risks that compress profit margins, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: UNRATE declines do not predict negative 5-day returns during post-shock recovery periods, or the signal direction flips to positive in this regime.
